candidates.row_label: Net Foreign Exchange Income - Business Revenue
candidates.why: This is clearly only FX-related revenue, not total revenue; no proper total revenue/sales line is available among candidates, and the other candidate is a small balancing adjustment. Using either would misrepresent top-line revenue.
This is a small balancing adjustment, not an actual revenue line; selecting it as total revenue would be incorrect. No suitable total revenue/sales line is present in the candidate list.

variables.notes: Total/top-line revenue (e.g., Net Interest Income + fee/commission income, etc.) exists in the statement but is not among the REVT candidates. Neither FX revenue nor a small balancing item is an appropriate proxy, so REVT is left empty and flagged for manual review.
Banking income statement does not have a conventional COGS line among candidates; acceptable to treat as 0 for PROF.

candidates.row_label: Net commissions and other operating income
candidates.why: This is operating income (revenues/fees), not an overhead expense. It should not be treated as SG&A.
This is classified as income, not expense, so it is not SG&A.
Same as previous: an income line, not SG&A overhead.
Represents staff/personnel costs, a core SG&A component for a bank. Fits SG&A definition and is clearly not a total or balancing item.
Represents non-personnel operating overhead (e.g., admin, selling, general expenses). It is a component expense line and not labeled as a total; appropriate SG&A component.

variables.notes: Selected the clear overhead expense components (personnel and other operating expenses). Excluded income lines from SG&A.
No explicit research & development expense is present among candidates; treated as 0 for PROF.

candidates.why: This is interest income, not expense; not appropriate for XINT, which should capture interest expense.
These are interest/fee income on loans, not interest expense.
Interest/dividend income, not expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest & Dividend Income - Investment Securities Available for Sale/Held to Maturity
candidates.why: Interest/dividend income on securities, not interest expense.
This appears to be the aggregate interest expense line for the bank, combining various borrowing and customer interest costs. It is the best single representation of XINT.
Component of interest expense, but using this together with the total would double count; better to use the comprehensive total ‘Interest Expenses’.

candidates.row_label: Interest on debt to credit institutions
candidates.why: Another component of interest expense; again, prefer the comprehensive total line.
Customer interest expense component; already captured within ‘Interest Expenses’.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Specific lease interest expense component; included in total interest expense line, so not chosen separately.
Represents non-controlling interest share of profit, not an interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Hybrid Capital Owners share of profit after tax (Interest on hybrid)
candidates.why: This is economically an interest-like distribution on hybrid capital, but is post-tax and separate from operating interest expense. For XINT, the main pre-tax ‘Interest Expenses’ line is preferred.

candidates.row_label: Profit after tax for non-controlling ownership interest
candidates.why: Allocation of profit to NCI, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; likely included in ‘Interest Expenses’. To avoid double counting, use the main total line only.
Balance sheet asset line, unrelated to interest expense.
Equity section item, not an interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; equity, not interest expense.

variables.notes: Chose the aggregate ‘Interest Expenses’ line as the best representation of total interest expense (XINT).

candidates.row_label: Dividends from shares and other equity instruments
candidates.why: Income statement dividend income; not an equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Earnings/diluted earnings per average equity certificate (in NOK)
candidates.why: Per-share earnings metric, not a balance sheet equity amount.

candidates.row_label: Earnings/diluted earnings per equity certificate (in NOK)
candidates.why: EPS metric, not equity.
An asset investment line, not the firm’s own equity capital.
Component of equity (share capital) but not total book equity; better to use a total that includes reserves, etc.
Generic equity component; not clearly the full shareholders’ equity total.
Part of retained/other equity; not the complete total equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Allocated to dividends and other equity capital
candidates.why: An allocation within equity, not the total equity balance itself.

This is explicitly labeled as total equity capital. The first occurrence (25,920) appears to be equity attributable to owners of the parent (before non-controlling interests); the second (26,213) includes NCI. For BE (book equity attributable to shareholders), the owner-only total is appropriate, but both rows share the same label, so implementation must ensure the owner-only one is used.

variables.notes: There are two rows labeled ‘Total equity capital’: one (25,920) likely excludes non-controlling interests (owners’ equity), and a second (26,213) appears to include NCI. For BE, the owner-only figure should be used, but both share the same label text; this ambiguity warrants manual confirmation in the underlying data.

candidates.why: Income statement share of profit for minority interests; could be used, but for MIB we typically prefer the balance sheet carrying amount of non-controlling interests.

candidates.row_label: Profit after tax for non-controlling ownership interest
candidates.why: This is the profit allocated to NCI, not the balance sheet amount of minority interest. Prefer the balance sheet NCI for MIB.
Asset investment, not minority interest in consolidated subsidiaries.
This is the balance sheet line for non-controlling (minority) interests and best matches MIB.
Also labels the same NCI concept; either this or ‘Non-controlling interests’ would be acceptable. Prefer the more explicit modern term.

variables.notes: Selected the balance sheet ‘Non-controlling interests’ line as the minority interest (MIB) measure.
